retrieved_result: Line Break Line Break Definition of Line Break A line break is a poetic device that is used at the end of a line, and the beginning of the next line in a poem. It can be employed without traditional punctuation. Also, it can be described as a point wherein a line is divided into two halves. Sometimes, a line is divided into two halves.
Decision_Reason: The retrieved result discusses line breaks in poetry, not apps for improving study habits; unrelated to the query.
